summary: Surah Al-Mursalat opens with oaths (of the sent ones) affirming the certainty of the promised Day of Decision. It pictures cosmic upheaval, repeated warnings of “woe that Day to the deniers,” and reminds of signs in creation, human origin, and sustenance. The deniers are driven to a shadow of smoke that gives no shade, with sparks like castles and yellow camels; they cannot speak nor excuse themselves. In contrast, the pious are in shade, springs, and fruits: “Eat and drink in peace for what you did.” It rebukes those who neglect prayer and ends: after this, what will they believe in?
